**Instructions:**
1. Read the source file
2. Find the section header line: `==== {jjdo_muster}`
3. Extract everything AFTER that line, BEFORE the next `[[` anchor line (which is `[[jjdo_saddle]]`)
4. Do NOT include the `[[jjdo_muster]]` anchor, the annotation line, or the `==== {jjdo_muster}` header
5. Write the extracted content to the target file

The extracted content should start with something like:
```
List all
{jjdhr_heat_s}
with summary information.
```

Report what you extracted (first and last few lines) for verification.

   135‚Üí
   136‚Üí== Overview
   137‚Üí
   138‚ÜíThe
   139‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   140‚Üíis the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
   141‚ÜíIt tracks
   142‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
   143‚Üí(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
   144‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_s}
   145‚Üí(discrete actions).
   146‚ÜíEach
   147‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   148‚Üímaintains a history of
   149‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   150‚Üí(plan refinements).
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üí== Design Principles
   153‚Üí
   154‚Üí=== Exclusive JSON Ownership
   155‚Üí
   156‚ÜíAll access to the
   157‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   158‚Üígoes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
   159‚ÜíNo jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
   160‚ÜíThis is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.
   161‚Üí
   162‚ÜíThe rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
   163‚ÜíThe internet is full of bad bash.
   164‚ÜíRust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
   165‚ÜíThe compiler is tireless; humans are not.
   166‚Üí
   167‚Üí=== Git as Journal
   168‚Üí
   169‚ÜíThe
   170‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   171‚Üíremains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
   172‚ÜíEvery mutation creates a diffable commit.
   173‚ÜíGit owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.
   174‚Üí
   175‚ÜíThe
   176‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   177‚Üíhistory within each
   178‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   179‚Üíexists for convenient postmortem analysis.
   180‚ÜíGit has the full history, but
   181‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   182‚Üílet us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.
   183‚Üí
   184‚Üí=== Diff-Friendly Mutations
   185‚Üí
   186‚ÜíNew
   187‚Üí{jjdkr_tack_s}
   188‚Üíprepend to position 0 rather than append.
   189‚ÜíThis produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
   190‚ÜíSmall discipline, large cumulative benefit.
   191‚Üí
   192‚ÜíThe
   193‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
   194‚Üíarray contains full
   195‚Üí{jjdt_coronet_s}.
   196‚ÜíEach
   197‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   198‚Üíembeds its parent
   199‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   200‚Üí(redundant within a single
   201‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}).
   202‚ÜíThis redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.
   203‚Üí
   204‚Üí=== Crash-Safe Architecture
   205‚Üí
   206‚ÜíBash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
   207‚Üí{jjdx_cli} owns all
   208‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   209‚Üífile access - reading, transforming, and writing.
   210‚Üí{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
   211‚ÜíIf {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
   212‚Üí
   213‚ÜíThe pattern:
   214‚Üí[source,bash]
   215‚Üí----
   216‚Üílock_acquire
   217‚Üívvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
   218‚Üílock_release
   219‚Üí----
   220‚Üí
   221‚ÜíThis keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.
   222‚Üí
   223‚Üí=== Deterministic Serialization
   224‚Üí
   225‚ÜíImplementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
   226‚ÜíFor `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
   227‚ÜíThis ensures that reordering paces via
   228‚Üí{jjdo_rail}
   229‚Üíproduces minimal diffs ‚Äî only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.
   230‚Üí
   231‚Üí=== Identity Encoding
   232‚Üí
   233‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   234‚Üí(`‚Ç£` prefix) and
   235‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   236‚Üí(`‚Ç¢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
   237‚ÜíThe base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
   238‚ÜíInternally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
   239‚Üí
   240‚Üí=== Horse Racing Vocabulary
   241‚Üí
   242‚ÜíJob Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
   243‚ÜíThis is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.
   244‚Üí
   245‚Üí* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
   246‚Üí* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
   247‚Üí* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
   248‚Üí* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `‚Ç£`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
   249‚Üí* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `‚Ç¢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
   250‚Üí* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
   251‚Üí* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)
   252‚Üí
   253‚Üí== Types
   254‚Üí
   255‚Üí[[jjdt_firemark]]
   256‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
   257‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}::
   258‚ÜíThe identity of a
   259‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}.
   260‚ÜíFormat: `‚Ç£` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `‚Ç£AB`).
   261‚ÜíThe 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
   262‚ÜíUsed as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   263‚ÜíInput may omit the `‚Ç£` prefix; output always includes it.
   264‚Üí
   265‚Üí[[jjdt_coronet]]
   266‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
   267‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}::
   268‚ÜíThe identity of a
   269‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}.
   270‚ÜíFormat: `‚Ç¢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `‚Ç¢ABCDE`).
   271‚ÜíGlobally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
   272‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   273‚Üíencoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
   274‚ÜíUsed as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   275‚ÜíInput may omit the `‚Ç¢` prefix; output always includes it.
   276‚Üí+
   277‚Üí*Reassignment:* When a
   278‚Üí{jjdpr_pace}
   279‚Üíis moved between
   280‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
   281‚Üívia
   282‚Üí{jjdo_draft},
   283‚Üíthe old
   284‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   285‚Üíbecomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
   286‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   287‚Üí{jjdhm_seed}.
   288‚ÜíThe new
   289‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   290‚Üíembeds the destination
   291‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}.
   292‚Üí{jjdkr_tack}
   293‚Üíhistory is preserved, with a note recording the original
   294‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}.
   295‚Üí
   296‚Üí[[jjdt_silks]]
   297‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_string‚üß
   298‚Üí{jjdt_silks}::
   299‚ÜíA kebab-case display name.
   300‚ÜíUsed for human-readable identification of
   301‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_s}
   302‚Üíand
   303‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_s}.
   304‚ÜíPattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`
   305‚Üí
   306‚Üí== Records
   307‚Üí
   308‚Üí=== {jjdgr_gallops}
   309‚Üí
   310‚Üí[[jjdgr_gallops]]
   311‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
   312‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}::
   313‚ÜíThe root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
   314‚ÜíSingleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
   315‚Üí
   316‚Üí==== Members
   317‚Üí
   318‚Üí* [[jjdgm_seed]]
   319‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   320‚Üí{jjdgm_seed}
   321‚Üíis the next
   322‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   323‚Üíto allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
   324‚ÜíIncremented by
   325‚Üí{jjdo_nominate}.
   326‚Üí
   327‚Üí=== {jjdhr_heat}
   328‚Üí
   329‚Üí[[jjdhr_heat]]
   330‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_record_json‚üß
   331‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}::
   332‚ÜíA bounded initiative with coherent goals.
   333‚ÜíContains zero or more
   334‚Üí{jjdpr_pace_s}
   335‚Üírepresenting discrete actions.
   336‚ÜíKeyed in JSON by
   337‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}.
   338‚Üí
   339‚Üí==== Members
   340‚Üí
   341‚Üí* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
   342‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   343‚Üí{jjdhm_firemark}
   344‚Üíis the
   345‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   346‚Üíprimary key.
   347‚ÜíType:
   348‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}.
   349‚ÜíImplicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   350‚Üí
   351‚Üí* [[jjdhm_silks]]
   352‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   353‚Üí{jjdhm_silks}
   354‚Üíis the
   355‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   356‚Üídisplay name.
   357‚ÜíType:
   358‚Üí{jjdt_silks}.
   359‚Üí
   360‚Üí* [[jjdhm_created]]
   361‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   362‚Üí{jjdhm_created}
   363‚Üírecords when the
   364‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   365‚Üíwas nominated.
   366‚ÜíFormat: YYMMDD.
   367‚Üí
   368‚Üí* [[jjdhm_status]]
   369‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   370‚Üí{jjdhm_status}
   371‚Üíindicates the
   372‚Üí{jjdhr_heat_p}
   373‚Üílifecycle state.
   374‚ÜíValues:
   375‚Üí{jjdhe_racing},
   376‚Üí{jjdhe_stabled},
   377‚Üíor
   378‚Üí{jjdhe_retired}.
   379‚Üí
   380‚Üí* [[jjdhm_order]]
   381‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeated‚üß
   382‚Üí{jjdhm_order}
   383‚Üílists
   384‚Üí{jjdt_coronet_s}
   385‚Üíin execution sequence.
   386‚ÜíMay be reordered via
   387‚Üí{jjdo_rail}.
   388‚Üí
   389‚Üí* [[jjdhm_seed]]
   390‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   391‚Üí{jjdhm_seed}
   392‚Üíis the next
   393‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   394‚Üíindex to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
   395‚ÜíIncremented by
   396‚Üí{jjdo_slate}.
   397‚Üí
   398‚Üí* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
   399‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axr_member axd_required‚üß
   400‚Üí{jjdhm_paddock}
   401‚Üíis the path to the paddock file containing
   402‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   403‚Üícontext and background.
   404‚ÜíFormat: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
   405‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   406‚Üí(without `‚Ç£` prefix).
   407‚ÜíCreated by
   408‚Üí{jjdo_nominate}.
   409‚Üí
   410‚Üí==== Status Values
   411‚Üí
   412‚Üí[[jjdhe_racing]]
   413‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
   414‚Üí{jjdhe_racing}::
   415‚ÜíThe
   416‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   417‚Üíis actively being worked.
   418‚Üí
   419‚Üí[[jjdhe_stabled]]
   420‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axt_enum_value‚üß
   421‚Üí{jjdhe_stabled}::
   422‚ÜíThe
   423‚Üí{jjdhr_heat}
   424‚Üíis paused, not actively worked.

   600‚Üí== Serialization
   601‚Üí
   602‚Üí[[jjdz_charset]]
   603‚Üí{jjdz_charset}::
   604‚ÜíThe 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
   605‚ÜíUses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 ¬ß5):
   606‚Üí`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
   607‚ÜíPosition in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
   608‚ÜíThis alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
   609‚ÜíMatches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.
   610‚Üí
   611‚Üí[[jjdz_encoding]]
   612‚Üí{jjdz_encoding}::
   613‚ÜíThe algorithm for encoding
   614‚Üí{jjdt_firemark}
   615‚Üíand
   616‚Üí{jjdt_coronet}
   617‚Üívalues.
   618‚Üí+
   619‚Üí*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
   620‚Üí+
   621‚Üí. Format: `‚Ç£` + 2 base64 characters
   622‚Üí. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `‚Ç£` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
   623‚Üí. To decode `‚Ç£XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
   624‚Üí+
   625‚Üí*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
   626‚Üí+
   627‚Üí. Format: `‚Ç¢` + 5 base64 characters
   628‚Üí. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
   629‚Üí. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
   630‚Üí. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `‚Ç¢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
   631‚Üí. To decode `‚Ç¢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
   632‚Üí+
   633‚Üí*Input flexibility:*
   634‚Üí+
   635‚ÜíCLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
   636‚ÜíLength determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
   637‚ÜíOutput always includes the prefix (`‚Ç£` or `‚Ç¢`).

   825‚Üí== CLI
   826‚Üí
   827‚Üí[[jjdx_vvx]]

   836‚ÜíThe Job Jockey command group within
   837‚Üí{jjdx_vvx}.
   838‚ÜíInvoked as `vvx jjx`.
   839‚ÜíAll operations are subcommands of

   842‚Üí
   843‚Üí== Commit Message Architecture
   844‚Üí
   845‚ÜíAll JJ commits include an action code that serves multiple purposes:
   846‚Üí- **Parsing steeplechase history**: Distinguishes between different operation types
   847‚Üí- **Semantic clarity**: Indicates the nature and context of each commit
   848‚Üí- **Consistency**: Applies uniformly across pace-level and heat-level operations
   849‚Üí
   850‚Üí== Routines
   851‚Üí
   852‚ÜíRoutines are internal reusable procedures invoked by operations.
   853‚ÜíThey encapsulate common logic for consistent behavior across the codebase.
   854‚Üí
   855‚Üí[[jjdr_load]]
   856‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axo_routine‚üß
   857‚Üí=== {jjdr_load}
   858‚Üí
   859‚ÜíLoad and validate the
   860‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   861‚Üífile.
   862‚ÜíEvery operation that reads
   863‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   864‚Üíinvokes this routine.
   865‚ÜíReturns a validated
   866‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   867‚Üíonly after all checks pass.
   868‚Üí
   869‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
   870‚Üí
   871‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
   872‚Üí* {jjda_file}
   873‚Üí
   874‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
   875‚Üí
   876‚Üí. Read file contents as raw bytes
   877‚Üí. Deserialize bytes into
   878‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   879‚Üístructure
   880‚Üí. Reserialize
   881‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   882‚Üístructure to JSON string
   883‚Üí. Compare reserialized bytes against original bytes
   884‚Üí. If different:
   885‚Üí{axc_fatal}
   886‚Üíwith diagnostic showing first difference location
   887‚Üí. Validate semantic constraints (timestamps, kebab-case silks, invariants)
   888‚Üí. If validation fails:
   889‚Üí{axc_fatal}
   890‚Üíwith validation errors
   891‚Üí. Return validated
   892‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   893‚Üí
   894‚Üí{jjds_stdout} None (returns structure to caller)
   895‚Üí
   896‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on validation failure or I/O error.
   897‚Üí
   898‚Üí*Architectural constraint:*
   899‚Üí{jjdr_load}
   900‚Üíis the ONLY way to obtain a
   901‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   902‚Üífrom disk.
   903‚ÜíThis makes validation bypass architecturally impossible.
   904‚ÜíThe round-trip check (steps 3-5) ensures the file is in canonical form.
   905‚ÜíThe semantic validation (steps 6-7) ensures business rules are satisfied.
   906‚Üí
   907‚Üí[[jjdr_save]]
   908‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axo_routine‚üß
   909‚Üí=== {jjdr_save}
   910‚Üí
   911‚ÜíPersist
   912‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   913‚Üíto disk with validation.
   914‚ÜíEvery operation that writes
   915‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   916‚Üíinvokes this routine.
   917‚ÜíValidates output by loading it back before finalizing.

   956‚Üí[[jjdr_persist]]

   959‚Üí
   960‚ÜíPersist
   961‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}

   963‚ÜíThis routine combines

   976‚Üí*
   977‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
   978‚Üí(in-memory structure to persist)
   979‚Üí

   992‚Üí
   993‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß

   995‚Üí
   996‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
   997‚Üí
   998‚Üí.
   999‚Üí{jjdr_save}
  1000‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
  1001‚Üíto
  1002‚Üí{jjda_file}
  1003‚Üí. Stage gallops file with `git add {file}`
  1004‚Üí. If paddock file exists for this identity, stage it with `git add {paddock_file}`
  1005‚Üí. Run size guard check with provided size_limit
  1006‚Üí. Determine action code:

  1010‚Üí.. If identity is

  1019‚Üí{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on error.

  1065‚Üí[[jjdr_wrap]]
  1066‚Üí// ‚ü¶axl_voices axo_routine‚üß
  1067‚Üí=== {jjdr_wrap}
  1068‚Üí
  1069‚ÜíMark a pace complete and commit all changes in one self-contained operation.
  1070‚ÜíThis routine combines staging, committing, state transition, and chalk marker creation.
  1071‚ÜíUsed by `/jjc-pace-wrap` to provide a single-command wrap experience.
  1072‚Üí
  1073‚Üí{jjds_arguments}
  1074‚Üí
  1075‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_required‚üß
  1076‚Üí* {jjdt_coronet}
  1077‚Üí(positional) ‚Äî pace to wrap
  1078‚Üí
  1079‚Üí// ‚ü¶axd_optional‚üß
  1080‚Üí* `--size-limit` ‚Äî maximum commit size in bytes (default: 50KB)
  1081‚Üí
  1082‚Üí{jjds_behavior}
  1083‚Üí
  1084‚Üí. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  1085‚Üí. Stage all changes (`git add -A`)
  1086‚Üí. Size guard check:
  1087‚Üí.. Get staged content size
  1088‚Üí.. If over limit: release lock, exit 2
  1089‚Üí. Generate commit message via Claude from diff
  1090‚Üí. Commit staged changes
  1091‚Üí. Transition pace state to complete via
  1092‚Üí{jjdo_tally}
  1093‚Üí. Create W chalk marker with outcome summary
  1094‚Üí. Release lock
  1095‚Üí. Return commit hash
  1096‚Üí
  1097‚Üí{jjds_exit_enumerated}
  1098‚Üí
  1099‚Üí* 0 ‚Äî success
  1100‚Üí* 1 ‚Äî general error (parse failure, gallops error, git error)
  1101‚Üí* 2 ‚Äî size guard exceeded (use --size-limit to override)
  1102‚Üí
  1103‚Üí*Design rationale:*
  1104‚Üí{jjdr_wrap}
  1105‚Üíconsolidates the multi-step wrap workflow into a single vvx command.
  1106‚ÜíThis eliminates the need for slash commands to orchestrate separate notch, tally, and chalk operations.
  1107‚ÜíThe distinct exit code 2 for size guard allows callers to advise users about the --size-limit flag.
  1108‚Üí
  1109‚Üí== Operations
  1110‚Üí
  1111‚ÜíOperations are implemented as {jjdx_cli} subcommands.
  1112‚Üí{jjdx_cli} owns all
  1113‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}
  1114‚Üífile access through
  1115‚Üí{jjdr_load}
  1116‚Üíand
  1117‚Üí{jjdr_save}.
  1118‚ÜíBash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
  1119‚Üí{jjda_file}.

  2554‚Üí=== Steeplechase Operations
  2555‚Üí
  2556‚ÜíThe steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
  2557‚Üí{jjdgr_gallops}

  2560‚Üí{jjdo_rein}

  2562‚Üí
  2563‚Üí==== Commit Message Patterns
  2564‚Üí
  2565‚ÜíAll JJ commits use the format: `jjb:HALLMARK:IDENTITY:ACTION: message`
  2566‚Üí
  2567‚Üí* `jjb` ‚Äî JJ brand prefix (literal)
  2568‚Üí* `HALLMARK` ‚Äî Version identifier. Format: NNNN (installed) or NNNN-xxxxxxx (dev). Source: `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` (installed) or max hallmark from `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/vovr_registry.json` + git HEAD (Kit Forge)
  2569‚Üí* `IDENTITY` ‚Äî ‚Ç¢CORONET for pace-level, ‚Ç£FIREMARK for heat-level
  2570‚Üí* `ACTION` ‚Äî single-letter code (required for all commits)
  2571‚Üí
  2572‚Üí*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
  2573‚Üí----
  2574‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç¢XXXXX:n: commit message
  2575‚Üí
  2576‚ÜíCo-Authored-By: ...
  2577‚Üí----
  2578‚Üí
  2579‚ÜíStandard notch commits use `n` to denote routine progress tracking.
  2580‚Üí
  2581‚Üí*Pace-level chalk* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
  2582‚Üí----
  2583‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç¢XXXXX:A: description
  2584‚Üí
  2585‚ÜíCo-Authored-By: ...
  2586‚Üí----
  2587‚Üí
  2588‚ÜíPace-level action codes:
  2589‚Üí
  2590‚Üí* `n` ‚Äî Notch: standard commit
  2591‚Üí* `A` ‚Äî APPROACH: proposed approach before work begins
  2592‚Üí* `W` ‚Äî WRAP: pace completion summary
  2593‚Üí* `F` ‚Äî FLY: autonomous execution began (bridled pace)
  2594‚Üí* `d` ‚Äî discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)
  2595‚Üí
  2596‚Üí*Heat-level operations*:
  2597‚Üí----
  2598‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:N: heat-silks
  2599‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:S: pace-silks
  2600‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:r: reordered
  2601‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:T: pace-silks
  2602‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:D: old-coronet ‚Üí new-coronet
  2603‚Üíjjb:HALLMARK:‚Ç£XX:R: heat-silks
  2604‚Üí----
  2605‚Üí
  2606‚ÜíHeat-level action codes:
  2607‚Üí
  2608‚Üí* `N` ‚Äî Nominate: create new heat
  2609‚Üí* `S` ‚Äî Slate: add new pace
  2610‚Üí* `r` ‚Äî rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
  2611‚Üí* `T` ‚Äî Tally: add tack to pace
  2612‚Üí* `D` ‚Äî Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
  2613‚Üí* `R` ‚Äî Retire: archive heat (uppercase)
